text: The Tavern is everything a tavern should be: smoky, low-ceilinged, and full of people who are either starting something or finishing it. The barkeep knows most of their names and has learned not to ask about the rest. A fire has burned in the hearth every day for forty years. The same cannot be said for all the regulars.
Dice rattle on a table near the window. Someone loses; someone else is careful not to look too pleased about it.
A merchant swears he saw lights moving in the dungeon last week. Not torchlight — something else. He has stopped going near the entrance road.
Word from the city guard: the creatures in the deeper levels have been louder of late. The duty captain doubled the watch on the culverts, just in case.
A fisherman pulled something up from the river that he could not identify. He threw it back immediately. He has not been back to the river since.
The tax collector's office is apparently understaffed this year. That's always been when the rules get enforced hardest, not least.
